summary: Frontend regression for the login race-condition fix verified. All scenarios PASS: admin/editor/viewer can sign in, dashboard renders, session persists for 10+ seconds without bouncing to /login, and survives a full page reload (F5). Logout returns to /login and protected route /dashboard properly redirects to /login when unauthenticated. Wrong-password shows login-error and does not log in. Opportunities table loads 18 seeded rows for the admin (confirming the auth cookie is sent on subsequent org-scoped API calls). Server-side RBAC verified: admin sees New/Pull/Verify + Admin + Settings; editor sees New/Pull/Verify but NOT Admin/Settings; viewer sees none of them.
action_items: None for the reported bug — the AuthContext race-condition fix (authedRef guard) and the axios 20s timeout resolve the reported sign-in issue. Login + session-persistence + reload all verified.
critical_code_review_comments: AuthContext.js — authedRef.current guard correctly prevents the late /auth/me 401 from clobbering a successful login. login()/register() set authedRef BEFORE setUser so a concurrent in-flight bootstrap response can't fire setUser(false). Logout resets authedRef to false. Logic is correct.
api.js timeout=20000 is reasonable; the bootstrap /auth/me cannot freeze the spinner indefinitely.

Minor: the review-request listed test-ids opps-new-btn / opps-pull-btn / opps-verify-btn that do not exist in code (actual ids: new-opp-button / pull-sam-button / verify-refresh-button). Consider either renaming for consistency or correcting documentation; not a bug.

success_rate.backend: n/a (not retested this iteration; iteration_1 was 28/28)

test_credentials: admin@govcon.io / Admin#2026 ; editor@govcon.io / Editor#2026 ; viewer@govcon.io / Editor#2026 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing seed (Orbital Defense Systems, 18 opportunities visible in admin view).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login race-condition bug is fixed. Verified flows: (1) admin/editor/viewer login -> dashboard (kpi-active-pursuits visible) -> still on dashboard after 10s -> reload preserves session; (2) logout -> /login and /dashboard redirects to /login; (3) wrong password -> login-error 'Invalid email or password'; (4) opportunities-table loads 18 rows (cookie carried on org-scoped calls); (5) RBAC: admin all visible, editor sees actions but not admin/settings, viewer sees none. Real test-ids in Opportunities.js are new-opp-button / pull-sam-button / verify-refresh-button (not the opps-*-btn names in the request). Auth network during reload: two GET /auth/me 200s (StrictMode double-fire) — harmless because authedRef guards against late clobber.
rca_of_the_issue: RCA: The reported 'cannot sign in' was a client-side race in AuthProvider. On mount the bootstrap useEffect issued GET /auth/me (which 401s when unauthenticated). If the user submitted credentials and POST /auth/login resolved BEFORE the in-flight /auth/me 401 returned, the 401 handler then called setUser(false), wiping the just-logged-in user and bouncing them back to /login (or freezing on the spinner if the bootstrap was still pending while Protected rendered FullLoader). Mitigation (applied by main agent and verified by this iteration): introduce authedRef in AuthContext; login()/register()/refreshUser() set authedRef=true before calling setUser; the bootstrap .then/.catch both no-op if authedRef.current is already true. Also added axios timeout:20000 in api.js so a hung bootstrap cannot keep Protected stuck on FullLoader forever. Reproduction confirmed pre-fix via the prior report; post-fix all six review scenarios pass.
